segments.text: Hello, I'm Johannes, a VP of code
security at Sonar and I'm going to talk
about code security in the AI area. We
will be covering the state of code
security where we are at then we look at
how Sona is approaching this challenge
today and then what are the capabilities
in to support this I will
show some demos and also give you a
sneak peek on what else we're working
on before I jump to a summary let's dive
into the state of code security so we
released a report here a research
study where we covered almost 8
billion lines of code from 1 million
developers that wrote code in 40,000
different organizations, right? So quite
a diverse set of code in different
programming languages and with 400
million code issues that we studied
here. And one finding was that almost
one issue every 800 lines of code
appears, right? That accounts to one to
two security issues per developer per
month. And you can see that still quite
a lot of security issues. When we study
the top security issue categories,
you can see that still the issue types
that you have been seeing in the last
decade are are fully present. Log
injections, cross-site scripting, SQL
injections, hard-coded secrets are
among those security issue types.
And now as we talked
about previously, right, the AI
accelerates this challenge. With AI,

that is written on top of that and
written faster we will see much more

So why is adding AI adding additional

right good examples here are these slop
squatting bugs where AI introduces

and then attackers can register them
and take over your code. And then we
also see that code is less efficient.
That means the leading LLMs are
solving programming tasks very well but
they are writing more verbose lines and
more complex code and that is a security
problem because now this code is harder
to maintain. More lines of code means
potentially more issues. And also if

code.
And on top of this the challenge
for organizations now is that you have

the cyber resilience act in the

basically that secure by design
development principles are applied that

in September 2026. The same applies

you also have to comply to use
continuous security risk assessment

Sonar is adding a unified verification

so critical because if you think

in this tool and also agents

we are making sure with that hook

Owasp top 10 for mobile for LLM etc.
